question_text: An electric motor lifts a 50 kg mass vertically upwards at a constant speed of 0.5 m/s. The motor is connected to a 230 V power supply and draws a current of 2 A. Calculate the efficiency of the motor. (Use g = 10 m/s²)
explanation: First, find electrical power input: P = IV = 230 × 2 = 460 W. Next, calculate the useful power output needed to lift the mass at constant speed using F = mg = 50 × 10 = 500 N, and P_useful = F × v = 500 × 0.5 = 250 W. Efficiency = (useful output ÷ total input) × 100% = (250 ÷ 460) × 100% ≈ 54.3%. The remaining energy is lost as heat and friction.
